name: Oregon Community
description_short: Member-owned credit union based in Eugene, Oregon, serving 260,000+ members across 28 Oregon counties and Washington with personal and business banking services.
description_long:
Oregon Community Credit Union (OCCU) is a non-profit credit union founded on June 25, 1956, originally as Lane Federal Credit Union. The organization has undergone several name changes—operating as U-Lane-O Credit Union from 1981 to 2003, then Oregon Community Credit Union until 2016 when it became known as OCCU. The credit union is headquartered in Eugene, Oregon, and operates 14 branch locations across its service area.

OCCU offers comprehensive personal and business banking services to its member-owned base of over 260,000 members. The credit union provides traditional banking products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and business banking solutions. As a community-chartered institution, OCCU expanded membership eligibility to anyone living or working within a 10-county area, democratizing access beyond its original employment-based membership model.

OCCU distinguishes itself through significant community engagement and philanthropic commitment. The organization established the OCCU Foundation in 2018, which has become a major charitable force—donating nearly $3 million as of 2023, including funding close to $1 million in student scholarships. The credit union has earned recognition from Portland Business Journal for Top 25 Corporate Philanthropy and Top 25 Most Admired Companies, plus recognition from Oregon Business Magazine as one of the Top 100 Best Companies to Work for in Oregon.

Financially, OCCU is a substantial institution with total assets of $3.405 billion and net income of $27.3 million. The credit union operates as a member-owned cooperative, meaning profits benefit members rather than external shareholders. However, detailed information about loan rates, fees, account minimums, and specific product offerings is limited in publicly available sources, requiring prospective members to visit myoccu.org for complete service specifications. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Charitable giving and community grants (through OCCU Foundation)
pros: Member-owned non-profit structure means profits support member benefits rather than external shareholders
Extensive service area covering 28 Oregon counties and Washington with 14 physical branch locations
Substantial charitable foundation (OCCU Foundation) donating nearly $3M annually including $1M in student scholarships
Large member base of 260,000+ provides stability and negotiating power with service providers
Recognized as Top 25 Most Admired Companies by Portland Business Journal and Top 100 Best Companies to Work for in Oregon
Strong financial position with $3.4 billion in total assets and $27.3 million net income
Community charger status allows membership for anyone living or working within service area, not just employees
cons: Limited geographic footprint compared to national banks—only serves Oregon counties and Washington
Specific loan rates, APRs, and fee structures not disclosed in available sources
Only 14 branch locations may limit convenience for members outside Eugene metropolitan area
No information provided about digital banking capabilities, mobile app features, or online services
Less brand recognition nationally compared to major national banks and credit unions
best_for: Oregon residents and Washington residents living/working in OCCU service area seeking community-focused banking
Members prioritizing philanthropic impact and community reinvestment over maximum returns
Small business owners in Oregon counties seeking relationship-based business banking
Customers valuing member-ownership and cooperative financial structure over shareholder-driven institutions
diagnosis: OCCU is best for Oregon and Washington residents seeking traditional banking services from a financially stable, community-focused credit union with strong philanthropic commitment. The primary caveat is limited geographic availability—membership requires living or working in the 28-county Oregon service area or Washington, making it inaccessible for out-of-region consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
